methods.description: Process for collecting pollen grains from coconut plants and storing them under various conditions to assess viability.

steps.description: Representative plants of Brazil Green Dwarf (BGD), Brazilian Tall (BRA) and Cameroon Red Dwarf (CRD) coconut accessions were selected from the Coconut Active Germplasm Bank of Embrapa Coastal Tablelands, Sergipe State.
steps.searchable_summary: select representative coconut plants from germplasm bank

steps.description: For each plant, a spathe close to maturation was marked before opening, removed from the plants and maintained at room temperature in the laboratory (28 ± 1°C) until anthesis.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and store spathes at room temperature until anthesis

steps.description: The pollen grains were collected, placed in cryotubes and maintained under the following storage conditions: T1, refrigerator (-4°C); T2, freezer (-20°C); T3, ultra-freezer (-80°C) and T4, liquid nitrogen (-196°C).
steps.searchable_summary: store pollen grains under various temperature conditions

methods.description: Method to assess the viability of pollen grains using acetic carmine staining and microscopy.

steps.description: The samples were removed from the storage conditions and subjected to rehydration for two hours in a moist chamber.
steps.searchable_summary: rehydrate pollen samples in moist chamber

steps.description: To determine the viability of the pollen grains by a staining method, a pollen sample of approximately 0.02 grams was placed on a slide, and a drop of 1% acetic carmine was added, followed by homogenization.
steps.searchable_summary: stain pollen samples with acetic carmine

steps.description: The slide was then placed in a Petri dish and incubated in a biological incubator at 37 ± 1°C for 25-30 minutes.
The slides were analyzed for the number of viable and nonviable pollen grains per quadrant using a microscope at 10x magnification and a digital camera.

methods.method_name: Pollen Viability Assessment by In Vitro Germination
methods.description: Method to assess the viability of pollen grains through in vitro germination on a culture medium.

steps.description: The pollen samples were inoculated onto Petri dishes containing 2 mL of the culture medium of Lora et al. (2006).
steps.searchable_summary: inoculate pollen samples on culture medium

steps.description: The Petri dishes were kept in an incubator for 24 hours at a temperature of 24 ± 1°C.
The Petri dishes were analyzed for the number of germinated pollen grains using a microscope at 10x magnification with a digital camera.

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the viability data of pollen grains under different storage conditions.

steps.description: The mean viability percentage of the pollen grains by staining with acetic carmine and in vitro germination under different storage conditions and the control were subjected to an analysis of variance.

The mean viability percentage of the pollen grains by both techniques after 30 and 60 days under different storage conditions were compared using the Tukey test at a 5% probability with SISVAR software.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Tukey test on viability data

steps.description: For the viability percentage by in vitro germination, the control was compared to all of the treatments using Dunnett's t-test at a 5% probability with SAS software.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Dunnett's t-test on germination data
